coordinate_provenance_records.original_place_text: Africa to Europe and Asia donkey spread context
coordinate_provenance_records.resolved_place_text: North Africa and Levant dispersal extent
coordinate_provenance_records.geocoder_or_gazetteer: not applied because the current lead is a broad spread transect
coordinate_provenance_records.confidence_rationale: The local archive description supports Africa-to-Eurasia spread, not one donkey site with defensible point geometry.

site_evidence_records.site_label: North African donkey domestication and spread transect
site_evidence_records.exact_source_text: Donkeys were domesticated once in Africa ~5,000 BCE, before rapidly spreading and differentiating into Europe and Asia ~2,500 BCE.
site_evidence_records.chronology_text: ~5000-2500 BCE donkey domestication and spread context
site_evidence_records.interpretation_note: This is a broad comparator domestication transect rather than one point-sized excavation context.
site_evidence_records.support_gap_note: No local paper is pinned yet for this project, so the current evidence row relies on the captured project description rather than a paper body or supplement.

normalization_bundle.normalization_scope: Non-human normalization currently governs accession-backed sample master rows, project summaries, study summaries, and curated locality summaries. Site and chronology extraction still vary by accession and remain explicit in sample inclusion status rather than being silently flattened.
